_doc: ASCII-art COMPOSITIONS + their tiles — the source the seed generator (scripts/gen-tileset-seeds.mjs) pushes into the DB tileset (nebulith .../ascii.json). Every rich asset is a COMPOSITION: a group of stacked CELLS/BLOCKS (dx,dy footprint + level = height, minecraft-style) each PAINTED with one tile. Combined they form the art. ASCII tiles and emoji PNG tiles are the SAME thing at this level — both paint blocks — so they render through the ONE generic block path (drawIsoTileBlock): a cell at (dx,dy,level) becomes a real iso CUBE with its glyph on the faces, individually SELECTABLE (heightLevel>=1 || height>=1). trunk tiles block movement; leaf/canopy tiles are WALKABLE (overhead canopy — you walk under the tree), so a tree occupies only its trunk cell. See MAP-MODEL.md §4/§5/§8 + TILE-VOCABULARY-CONTRACT.

tree_small._doc: The reference tree — pure lego stacking, no shape logic. A 3-cell TRUNK at the anchor (levels 0-1-2, dy0) with a CANOPY that widens DOWN the levels (5 at L3, 3 at L4, 1 crown at L5) AND is 3 DEEP: the whole leaf section is repeated at dy -1, 0, +1 so the tree has depth in iso (3 leaf sections next to each other, one trunk), not a flat leaning triangle. Each cell is a block painted with a leaf/trunk tile (ascii glyph or emoji per active style); the canopy is walkable overhead, so the tree blocks only its trunk cell.

tree_dead._doc: A DEAD tree — same lego model as tree_small, but leafless: a 3-cell TRUNK (L0-1-2) topped by BARE BRANCHES (snag tiles spread at L3 across dx -1/0/1, plus a crown snag at L4). It reads as a bare-branched dead tree, NOT an empty trunk column. The snag branches are walkable overhead, so the dead tree blocks only its trunk cell. Used where DEAD_TREE_CHANCE fires (autumn/winter/lava dead wood).
